language_coverage_note: EN and ZH come from OpenHowNet's bundled BabelNet (Qi 2019/2022). JA and KO are EMPTY across all 15,755 synsets in the bundle вҖ” the API exposes ja_synonyms/ko_synonyms fields but the underlying data has no entries. JA/KO candidates must be CONSTRUCTED by native-speaker collaborators using EN/ZH as anchors, per docs/design/alignment_protocol.md.

schema_documentation.release_scope: EN/ZH BabelNet-harvested candidates bundled with sparse-language flags.
schema_documentation.harvest_method: EN and ZH candidate lists are automatically harvested from the OpenHowNet bundle (Dong & Dong 2010; Qi et al. 2019), which packages BabelNet synsets (Qi et al. 2022). Candidates are not per-item annotated by human raters.
schema_documentation.contrast_with_JA_KO: JA/KO records in candidates_JA.jsonl and candidates_KO.jsonl are produced by trained linguist annotators native to each language; each candidate carries a three-way correspondence tag (full / partial / no_correspondence), a free-text register note, and source metadata. This annotation depth reflects the construction (rather than harvest) methodology.

field_mapping.candidates.EN_candidates: en_seed_examples (JA/KO carry only the seed subset used as anchor)
field_mapping.babelnet_synset_ids: (EN/ZH only) BabelNet synset IDs backing each node
field_mapping.sparse_languages: (EN/ZH only) languages with zero coverage in the bundle (always ['JA', 'KO'] here)
field_mapping.affect_polarity: polarity (JA/KO use 'polarity' for the same field)
field_mapping.role_hint: derived from source ontology role_edges; present in both EN/ZH (post-v1.1) and JA/KO records to provide HowNet structural grounding

schema_version_history.change: Initial release; role_hint absent from EN/ZH bundle.
Added role_hint field (derived from source ontology role_edges) and this schema_documentation block.
